description: Progressive market data cache: smart same-day append, eliminate redundant derived-interval storage, add observability logging

userStories.title: Add lastBarAt Column to candle_cache Schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a lastBarAt timestamp column on the candle_cache table so that the cache can quickly determine whether it needs to re-fetch without parsing the JSON bars blob.
acceptanceCriteria: Add lastBarAt column (timestamp with timezone, nullable for backward compat) to candle_cache in schema.ts
lastBarAt stores the timestamp of the last bar in the cached JSON array
Remove the '// No expiresAt' comment (no longer accurate given progressive model)
Run bun run db:push to verify schema change applies cleanly
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)

userStories.title: Implement Smart Append Logic in getOHLCBars
userStories.description: As a developer, I want getOHLCBars to detect when a same-day cache entry is stale and re-fetch from Databento to replace the cache entry, so that progressive data filling works correctly across multiple user imports throughout the day.
acceptanceCriteria: On cache hit, compare lastBarAt against current time: if date is today (UTC) and lastBarAt is more than 5 minutes old, re-fetch from Databento for the full day and replace the cache entry
For historical dates (before today UTC), keep current behavior — cache hit returns immediately, never re-fetches
Change onConflictDoNothing() to onConflictDoUpdate() — update bars, barCount, lastBarAt, and fetchedAt when re-fetching
Populate lastBarAt from the last bar's timestamp when writing to cache (both new inserts and updates)
Backfill logic: if an existing cache row has null lastBarAt (from before this change), parse the bars JSON to extract it, then proceed with staleness comparison
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Key design decision: on same-day re-fetch, replace the entire day's bars rather than merging partial ranges. Databento charges per request not per time range, so full re-fetch is simpler with no cost penalty. The getOHLCBars function currently takes (symbol, interval, date) — it does NOT know the trade's exit time. So staleness is determined by comparing lastBarAt against current time, not against any trade-specific time.

userStories.title: Eliminate Redundant Derived-Interval Cache Storage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to stop caching derived intervals (5min, 15min, 30min, 4h) in candle_cache so that we only store base data from Databento (1min, 1h) and compute derived intervals on the fly.
acceptanceCriteria: In getOHLCBars, when the requested interval is a derived interval (5min, 15min, 30min), fetch/cache the 1min base data instead, then aggregate before returning
When requested interval is 4h, fetch/cache the 1h base data, then aggregate before returning
Only 1min and 1h rows are ever written to candle_cache
The aggregateBars function in service.ts handles the conversion (already exists)
Existing derived-interval cache rows are harmlessly ignored (they just won't be read anymore)
Create a BaseInterval type ('1min' | '1h') distinct from CacheInterval to clarify storable vs requestable intervals
All three consumer endpoints (getChartData, getFullDayChartData, analyzePriceAction) continue working correctly
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: The aggregateBars function already exists in service.ts (lines 306-360). 5min/15min/30min derive from 1min bars. 4h derives from 1h bars.

userStories.title: Update getOHLCForChart and getOHLCForTimeRange for Base Interval Fetch
userStories.description: As a developer, I want getOHLCForChart and getOHLCForTimeRange to always fetch base interval data and aggregate to the requested interval, so chart data is consistent with the new cache-only-base-intervals approach.
acceptanceCriteria: getOHLCForChart fetches 1min bars internally regardless of requested interval, then aggregates to requested interval before returning
getOHLCForTimeRange similarly fetches base interval and aggregates if called with derived intervals
getFullDayBars already fetches 1min — verify no changes needed
Charts still display correctly (no timestamp or OHLC regressions)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: getFullDayBars (line 503) already hardcodes 1min. getOHLCForChart (line 234) currently passes the interval through — needs to be changed to always use 1min then aggregate.

userStories.title: Update marketData tRPC Router for New Cache Behavior
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the marketData tRPC router to work seamlessly with the progressive cache and base-interval-only storage.
acceptanceCriteria: getChartData endpoint: interval is still user-facing in schema, internally delegates to base interval fetch + aggregation via updated service functions
getFullDayChartData endpoint: already uses 1min, verify unchanged behavior
analyzePriceAction endpoint: uses 1min and 1h which are base intervals, verify still works
getOHLC endpoint (deprecated): update to use base-interval fetch + aggregation
CacheInterval type still includes all user-requestable intervals (1min, 5min, 15min, 30min, 1h, 4h) for API contracts
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: The router at src/server/api/routers/marketData.ts. Three chart components consume getFullDayChartData: running-pnl-tab.tsx, tradingview-chart.tsx, trade-replay.tsx.

userStories.title: Add Basic Cache Observability Logging
userStories.description: As a developer, I want basic console logging for cache hits, misses, and appends so that I can monitor cache behavior in development and production logs.
acceptanceCriteria: Log on cache hit (historical): [market-data] cache hit: ES 1min 2024-12-15 (387 bars)
Log on cache miss (new fetch): [market-data] cache miss: ES 1min 2024-12-15 → fetching from Databento
Log on smart append (same-day re-fetch): [market-data] cache refresh: ES 1min 2024-03-14 (was 240 bars → now 387 bars)
Log on derived interval aggregation: [market-data] aggregating: ES 1min → 5min (387 → 78 bars)
Use console.info with [market-data] prefix (consistent, greppable)
No external dependencies (no OpenTelemetry — just structured console logs)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)

userStories.title: Unit Tests for Aggregation and Staleness Detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for the bar aggregation and staleness detection logic so that we can verify correctness of derived interval computation and progressive cache behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file created: tests/unit/market-data-aggregation.test.ts

Tests for aggregateBars (service.ts version): 1min → 5min correct OHLC (first open, max high, min low, last close), 1min → 15min and 30min, 1h → 4h, empty input returns empty, single bar returns single bar
Tests for lastBarAt extraction: correctly identifies last bar timestamp from sorted bar array, handles empty bar array
Tests for staleness detection: historical date never stale, today with old lastBarAt is stale, today with recent lastBarAt is not stale
All tests pass (bun run test)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)

userStories.title: Integration Tests for Progressive Cache Behavior
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests verifying the smart append and base-interval-only caching with real database operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file created: tests/integration/market-data-cache.test.ts
Tests use Testcontainers PostgreSQL (real DB, not mocks)
Test: first insert for a date creates cache entry with correct lastBarAt
Test: second fetch for same historical date returns cached data without re-inserting
Test: onConflictDoUpdate correctly replaces rows when bars/barCount/lastBarAt/fetchedAt change
Test: only 1min and 1h interval values are written to candle_cache (no 5min/15min/30min/4h rows)
Test: requesting 5min interval returns correctly aggregated bars derived from 1min cache data
All tests pass (bun run test)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
userStories.notes: Integration tests should directly test the cache DB operations and aggregation pipeline. Mock the Databento API fetch to avoid real API calls in tests.

userStories.title: Cleanup Script to Delete Redundant Derived-Interval Cache Rows
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a one-time cleanup script to remove existing derived-interval rows from candle_cache to reclaim storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Create script at scripts/cleanup-derived-cache.ts
Deletes all candle_cache rows where interval is NOT 1min or 1h
Logs how many rows were deleted
Script is idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
Document in script header that this is a one-time cleanup
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
